# Review Cadence

**Flexibility: Expected (SHOULD)**

## The Standard

Every governed component SHOULD have a defined review cadence (in days) and a
last_reviewed date. When an object exceeds its review cadence, it is flagged
as stale. Stale objects receive reduced trust in retrieval and decision-making.

**Default cadences (tunable per venture):**

| Object Type | Default Cadence | Rationale |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|
| Concept | 180 days | Concepts are stable physics — slow change |
| Principle | 90 days | Commitments need regular reaffirmation |
| Standard | 90 days | Aspirational targets evolve with capability |
| Rule | 60 days | Testable compliance needs frequent verification |
| Contract | 60 days | Mechanical specs need currency |
| Decision | 365 days | Decisions are point-in-time records — review annually |
| Standing Order | 90 days | Operational governance needs regular review |

## What Good Looks Like

- Every governed component has a `review_cadence` and `last_reviewed` field.
- A staleness report can identify every object past its review date.
- Overdue reviews trigger notifications to the object's owner.
- Reviews are lightweight: confirm currency, update last_reviewed, note any changes needed.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-C07 (Knowledge Decay) — review cadence is the defense against decay
- JOS-R04 (Review Currency) — the testable enforcement of this standard
- JOS-P11 (Everything That Matters) — review cadence is essential metadata
